qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: CBOR decoder lacks nesting limits and crashes on deeply nested attacker input
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same `@stablelib/cbor` decoder root cause as the advisory: recursive descent in `packages/cbor/cbor.ts` without a maximum-depth guard, fixed upstream by commit `0149e18d9d4736e22c257744ca945ebce7899a01`.

selected_finding.title: CBOR decoder lacks nesting limits and crashes on deeply nested attacker input

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory's vulnerable code and root cause in `@stablelib/cbor`, and the copied PoC reproduced the advisory-described stack-exhaustion denial of service in the vulnerable decode path.
evidence: The advisory and finding both identify recursive CBOR decoding in `packages/cbor/cbor.ts` with no depth limit; the finding also cites the fixing upstream commit `0149e18d9d4736e22c257744ca945ebce7899a01` that adds `maxDepth`.

The copied PoC run at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_b7da709d/.judge/poc_runs/cbor_stack_exhaustion_dos/run.log` shows `Decoding nested CBOR payload: depth=12000 bytes=12001` immediately before an uncaught `RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded`.

That same run log contains recursive frames in `packages/cbor/lib/cbor.js` (`Decoder._decodeValue` and `Decoder._decodeArray`) and ends with `EVOHUNT_PROOF: stack exhaustion confirmed` plus `[+] Verification succeeded`.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied reproduction built the pinned vulnerable StableLib commit, decoded a 12,000-level nested array payload, emitted an uncaught `RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded` with recursive frames in `packages/cbor/lib/cbor.js`, and then printed `EVOHUNT_PROOF: stack exhaustion confirmed`.
